title: libpod/Container.rootFsSize(): use recorded image sizes
body:
In rootFsSize(), instead of calculating the size of the diff for every layer of the container's base image, ask the storage library for the sum of the values it recorded when it first wrote those layers.

In a similar fashion, teach rwSize() to use the library's ContainerSize() method instead of trying to roll its own.

Replace calls to pkg/util.SizeOfPath() with calls to github.com/containers/storage/pkg/directory.Size(), which does the same thing.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
`podman system df` now counts files which podman generates for use with specific containers as part of the disk space used by those containers, and which can be reclaimed by removing those containers.  It also counts space used by files it associates with specific images and volumes as being used by those images and volumes.
```

comments.body: Had to update a couple of integration tests to now always expect a non-zero size for a container that we know we generated a runtime config (and /etc/hosts, etc.) for, since we count all of that now.
Could've sworn I ran that test locally before pushing.  Revised.
